title: Boat Rental in Reykjavik on a Budget: Costs, Tips and Best Boats for 2026
html:
Renting a boat in Reykjavik need not break the bank. From Reykjavik Old Harbour, shared tours start at around 80€ per person, while self-drive motorboats begin at 350€ for a half-day. Compare verified options on the BoatHire24 marketplace to find a deal that suits your wallet.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Reykjavik?

Prices range from 80€ per person for a shared tour to over 2,500€ for a crewed yacht day. The cost depends on boat size, duration and season. Below is a typical price breakdown:

  • Shared whale-watching or puffin tour: 80, 120€ per person for 3 hours.
  • Small motorboat (up to 6 guests) half-day: 350, 600€.
  • Catamaran or sailing yacht full-day: 800, 1,500€ for up to 12 guests.
  • Crewed luxury yacht full-day: 1,800, 2,500€ for up to 8 guests.

For the best value, consider low-season bookings in May or September, or browse day charters in Reykjavik on BoatHire24 to compare prices.

Where to set off in Reykjavik

The main departure point is Reykjavik Old Harbour, with several marinas nearby. The Old Harbour is home to tour operators and private rentals, plus the nearby Reykjavik Marina offers berths for bigger yachts. Other launch spots include:

  • Reykjavik Old Harbour (central, most tours)
  • Reykjavik Marina (year-round moorings)
  • Kópavogur Fjord (south of the city, calm waters)
  • Hafnarfjörður Harbour (15 minutes south, historic port)
  • Akurey Island (bird-watching, close to the harbour)
  • Viðey Island (a popular sailing destination with clear views)

Which boat to rent

Your choice depends on group size, budget and whether you want a skipper. Below are recommended options:

  1. Rib or speedboat, best for a short coastal exploration, up to 6 guests, from 350€ half-day.
  2. Motor yacht (e.g. Azimut), ideal for a comfortable day cruise, up to 8 guests, from 1,000€ full-day. Check the Prestige 500 charter page for a similar model.
  3. Sailing yacht (e.g. Lagoon catamaran), perfect for a relaxed sail, up to 12 guests, from 800€ full-day.
  4. Luxury yacht (e.g. Sunseeker), for a premium experience, up to 8 guests, from 1,800€ full-day.

When to go and what about licences?

The high season runs from June to August, with southerly winds common. In 2026, expect calm conditions in July. For self-drive boats, you normally need an ICC or equivalent; the operator will confirm exact requirements upon booking.

Local tip: set off early from the Old Harbour to avoid afternoon gusts and to see puffins on Akurey Island before the tour boats arrive.

At a glance

  • Price band: 80€ per person (shared) to 2,500€ (crewed yacht)
  • Peak months: June, July, August
  • Trip length: 3 hours to full day
  • Guest capacity: 1 to 12 guests
  • Currency: EUR (euros)

Questions about boat rental in Reykjavik

Do I need a boat licence in Iceland?

For self-drive boats, an International Certificate of Competence (ICC) is usually required, but the operator can confirm the exact requirements when you book through BoatHire24.

How far is Viðey Island from the harbour?

Viðey Island lies about 4 nautical miles north of Reykjavik Old Harbour, a 20-30 minute boat ride depending on speed.

Can I rent a boat without a skipper?

Yes, many small motorboats and ribs are available for self-drive hire provided you have the relevant licence and experience.

What is the best month for calm seas?

July typically offers the calmest conditions, with average winds around 10 knots and long daylight hours.

Whether you are exploring the fjords on a budget or treating a group to a luxury charter, renting in Reykjavik is straightforward. To find the right boat or list your own vessel, visit the BoatHire24 marketplace and join a global community of boat owners and renters.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Reykjavik and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
topic: a budget guide to renting a boat in Reykjavik
